 {"id":"br-04qv0","title":"Attention Feed Phase 1c: Derive the first-class attention signals an operator agent actually cares about","description":"# Why this bead exists
A raw event feed alone is still too noisy for an operator agent. What matters is not every underlying change, but the first-class attention signals that tell the LLM whether judgment is needed now. This bead adds those derived signals without pretending to become the decision-maker.

# Mandatory v1 signal set
- `session_changed`
- `pane_changed`
- `agent_state_changed`
- `stalled`
- `context_hot`
- `rate_limited`
- `alert_raised`

# Scope
1. Derive operator-relevant signals from existing lifecycle, activity, context, and alert sources.
2. Set actionability/severity for each signal class so the system can distinguish passive updates from real attention demands.
3. Attach machine-usable details such as pane indices, idle durations, context ratios, or alert IDs.
4. Emit follow-up suggestions that point operators toward existing targeted commands rather than giant snapshots unless resync is actually needed.
5. Keep thresholds and heuristics explainable and testable; if a stall or rate-limit rule exists, it must be obvious where it comes from.
6. Record threshold rationale in comments/tests so future tuning does not become folklore.

# Guardrails
- This bead must not treat raw output volume as equivalent to importance.
- A signal can be `interesting` without being `action_required`.
- If a threshold is heuristic, say so in code comments/tests rather than burying it.

# Detailed verification expectations
- Synthetic timeline tests for stall detection.
- Context-pressure tests around threshold boundaries.
- Tests proving passive output churn does not automatically become `action_required`.
- Scenario fixtures for lifecycle transitions, rate-limit text detection, and alert promotion.
- Debug logging in tests that prints the exact reason a signal was emitted, suppressed, or downgraded.

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] The feed can wake an operator for real changes such as stalls, context pressure, or rate limits.
- [ ] Actionability classes are applied consistently across the mandatory v1 signal set.
- [ ] Signal payloads contain enough structured detail for precise follow-up.
- [ ] The implementation remains explainable rather than magical.
- [ ] Threshold rationale is visible to future maintainers.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:19.839224512Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T07:17:18.185206410Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T07:17:18.184690095Z","close_reason":"Code verified: attention_feed.go (64KB), attention_contract.go (31KB), attention_feed_test.go (31KB) all implement the mandatory v1 signal set","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["attention-feed","phase-1","robot","signals"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-04qv0","depends_on_id":"br-7v4ns","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:22.870372511Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-08fgf","title":"Phase 1a: Enable Mouse Support in Dashboard, Palette, and Tutorial","description":"# Enable Mouse Support Across All TUI Programs

## Background
NTM has 3 Bubble Tea programs (dashboard, palette, tutorial) that all use tea.WithAltScreen()
but NONE use tea.WithMouseCellMotion(). This is the single lowest-effort, highest-impact change
in the entire upgrade. Modern terminals (iTerm2, Ghostty, WezTerm, Windows Terminal, Kitty)
all support mouse events, and Bubble Tea handles them automatically.

## Current State (dashboard.go:7008)
  p := tea.NewProgram(model, tea.WithAltScreen())

## Target State
  p := tea.NewProgram(model, tea.WithAltScreen(), tea.WithMouseCellMotion())

## What tea.WithMouseCellMotion() Enables
- tea.MouseMsg events in Update() with position (X, Y), button, action (press/release/motion)
- Mouse wheel events (tea.MouseWheelUp, tea.MouseWheelDown)
- Click events for interactive elements
- Motion tracking for hover effects (cell-level granularity)

## Implementation Plan

### 1. Add mouse option to all 3 TUI programs:
- internal/tui/dashboard/dashboard.go:7008 (RunWithOptions)
- internal/cli/palette.go:112
- internal/cli/tutorial.go:60

### 2. Add tea.MouseMsg handler in dashboard Update():
- Click on pane list items: translate Y position to list index, select that pane
- Click on tab bar items: switch to that panel focus
- Mouse wheel in pane detail: scroll content up/down
- Click on toast: dismiss it
- Log mouse events at debug level: log.Printf("mouse: button=%d x=%d y=%d action=%d", ...)

### 3. Add tea.MouseMsg handler in palette Update():
- Click on command list items: select that command
- Mouse wheel: scroll command list

### 4. Add NTM_MOUSE env var control:
  if v, ok := os.LookupEnv("NTM_MOUSE"); ok && v == "0" {
      // Don't add WithMouseCellMotion — user opted out
  }
This allows disabling mouse if it conflicts with tmux mouse mode.

## Unit Tests (REQUIRED)

### Test file: internal/tui/dashboard/dashboard_mouse_test.go
  func TestMouseClickSelectsPane(t *testing.T) {
      m := newTestModel(3) // 3 panes
      // Simulate mouse click at Y position of pane 2
      m, _ = m.Update(tea.MouseMsg{X: 5, Y: 8, Button: tea.MouseButtonLeft, Action: tea.MouseActionPress})
      if m.(Model).selectedPane != 1 { t.Errorf("expected pane 1 selected, got %d", m.(Model).selectedPane) }
  }

  func TestMouseWheelScrollsDetail(t *testing.T) {
      m := newTestModel(3)
      // Focus on detail pane, scroll down
      m, _ = m.Update(tea.MouseMsg{Button: tea.MouseButtonWheelDown})
      // Verify scroll position changed
  }

  func TestMouseDisabledByEnvVar(t *testing.T) {
      t.Setenv("NTM_MOUSE", "0")
      // Verify mouse option not added
  }

### Test file: internal/cli/palette_mouse_test.go
  func TestPaletteMouseClick(t *testing.T) { ... }

## E2E / VHS Verification
Create testdata/vhs/dashboard-mouse.tape:
  Output testdata/vhs/output/dashboard-mouse.gif
  Set FontSize 16
  Set Width 1800
  Set Height 1000
  Require ntm
  Hide
  Type "ntm dashboard test"
  Enter
  Show
  Sleep 3s
  # Mouse events are complex in VHS; verify via unit tests instead
  Type "q"

Run TUI Inspector: capture_ntm_tui.sh --profile ntm-dashboard-navigate
Compare snapshot to Phase 0 baseline to ensure no visual regression.

## Logging
- Debug log all mouse events when NTM_DEBUG=1: coordinates, button, action
- Log when mouse click hits a recognized target vs empty space
- Log when mouse mode is disabled by NTM_MOUSE=0

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tea.WithMouseCellMotion() added to all 3 TUI programs
- [ ] Mouse click selects panes in dashboard pane list
- [ ] Mouse wheel scrolls in focused panels
- [ ] NTM_MOUSE=0 disables mouse support
- [ ] All new unit tests pass: go test -v -run TestMouse ./internal/tui/dashboard/
- [ ] All existing tests still pass: go test -short ./internal/tui/...
- [ ] No visual regression in VHS captures
- [ ] Mouse events don't interfere with tmux mouse mode (manual test)

## Files to Modify
- internal/tui/dashboard/dashboard.go (line 7008 + MouseMsg handler in Update)
- internal/cli/palette.go (line 112)
- internal/cli/tutorial.go (line 60)

## Files to Create
None — add mouse tests to existing dashboard_render_pure_test.go
(AGENTS.md: no file proliferation for tests that belong in existing files)

## Risk: LOW — Adding WithMouseCellMotion is additive. Unhandled mouse events are ignored.
NTM_MOUSE=0 escape hatch for tmux conflicts.

## PARALLEL EXECUTION NOTE
This bead and Phase 1b (br-h3z1b) both modify dashboard.go. Changes are in
NON-OVERLAPPING sections (1a: RunWithOptions + MouseMsg case; 1b: DashboardTickMsg
handler). If running in parallel agents, use Agent Mail file reservations to
coordinate, or merge sequentially. -{"id":"br-27cl0","title":"Phase 4b: Extend Harmonica Spring Animations to Focus, Progress, and Sparklines","description":"# Extend Harmonica Spring Animations to Focus, Progress, and Sparklines

## Background
Phase 1b wires Harmonica for toasts. This extends springs across ALL animation surfaces.
Estimate: 240m (4h). SpringManager ~100 LOC + 4 integrations ~30-50 LOC each + tests ~200 LOC.

## Animation Targets

### 1. Panel Focus Transition (requires Phase 3b focus ring)
  Focus indicator border opacity springs between 0 and 1.
  Spring: FPS(60), 10.0, 0.7 (fast, minimal overshoot)

### 2. Progress Bar Value Transitions
  displayPercent springs to targetPercent.
  Spring: FPS(60), 5.0, 0.3 (visible overshoot — satisfying bounce)
  DEAD ZONE: Only spring if |delta| > 2%. Prevents constant animation when values fluctuate
  slightly (e.g., context usage oscillating between 74% and 76%).

### 3. Sparkline Data Transitions
  Each value springs from previous to new.
  Spring: FPS(60), 8.0, 0.5 (moderate overshoot)

### 4. Scroll Position (with viewport from Phase 2c)
  Spring-animated momentum scrolling.
  Spring: FPS(60), 12.0, 0.6 (fast, slight deceleration)

## Spring Manager — NO MUTEX NEEDED
Bubble Tea is single-threaded (Update/View run on one goroutine). SpringManager doesn't
need sync.Mutex. Use a plain map:

  type SpringManager struct {
      springs map[string]*AnimatedFloat
  }
  type AnimatedFloat struct {
      Value, Velocity, Target float64
      Spring                  harmonica.Spring
      DeadZone                float64  // minimum delta to trigger animation
  }
  func (sm *SpringManager) Set(id string, target float64)
  func (sm *SpringManager) SetWithParams(id string, target, stiffness, damping float64)
  func (sm *SpringManager) Tick()
  func (sm *SpringManager) Get(id string) float64
  func (sm *SpringManager) IsAnimating() bool
  func (sm *SpringManager) IsSettled(id string) bool

## Tick Rate Management
  When IsAnimating() → 16ms tick. When settled → normal rate (100ms/500ms).
  Already partially solved by StateActive/StateIdle.

## ReducedMotion
  ALL springs: if ReducedMotionEnabled() { Value = Target, Velocity = 0 }

## Unit Tests (REQUIRED)

### Test file: internal/tui/components/spring_manager_test.go (new)
  func TestSpringManagerSetAndGet(t *testing.T) {
      sm := NewSpringManager()
      sm.Set("test", 10.0)
      if sm.Get("test") != 0.0 { t.Error("initial value should be 0") }
      for i := 0; i < 200; i++ { sm.Tick() }
      if math.Abs(sm.Get("test")-10.0) > 0.5 { t.Errorf("not settled: %f", sm.Get("test")) }
  }

  func TestSpringManagerIsAnimating(t *testing.T) {
      sm := NewSpringManager()
      if sm.IsAnimating() { t.Error("empty should not animate") }
      sm.Set("test", 10.0)
      if !sm.IsAnimating() { t.Error("should be animating") }
      for i := 0; i < 300; i++ { sm.Tick() }
      if sm.IsAnimating() { t.Error("should be settled") }
  }

  func TestSpringManagerDeadZone(t *testing.T) {
      sm := NewSpringManager()
      sm.springs["test"] = &AnimatedFloat{Value: 50.0, Target: 50.0, DeadZone: 2.0,
          Spring: harmonica.NewSpring(harmonica.FPS(60), 5.0, 0.3)}
      sm.Set("test", 51.0) // delta = 1.0 < dead zone 2.0
      if sm.IsAnimating() { t.Error("delta within dead zone should not animate") }
      sm.Set("test", 53.0) // delta = 3.0 > dead zone 2.0
      if !sm.IsAnimating() { t.Error("delta above dead zone should animate") }
  }

  func TestSpringManagerReducedMotion(t *testing.T) {
      t.Setenv("NTM_REDUCE_MOTION", "1")
      sm := NewSpringManager()
      sm.Set("test", 10.0)
      if sm.Get("test") != 10.0 { t.Error("instant in reduced motion") }
      if sm.IsAnimating() { t.Error("no animation in reduced motion") }
  }

  func TestSpringManagerMultiple(t *testing.T) {
      sm := NewSpringManager()
      sm.Set("a", 5.0); sm.Set("b", 10.0); sm.Set("c", 15.0)
      for i := 0; i < 300; i++ { sm.Tick() }
      for id, target := range map[string]float64{"a": 5, "b": 10, "c": 15} {
          if math.Abs(sm.Get(id)-target) > 0.5 { t.Errorf("%s not settled", id) }
      }
  }

  func TestSpringManagerOvershoot(t *testing.T) {
      sm := NewSpringManager()
      sm.SetWithParams("test", 10.0, 5.0, 0.3)
      max := 0.0
      for i := 0; i < 100; i++ {
          sm.Tick()
          if sm.Get("test") > max { max = sm.Get("test") }
      }
      if max <= 10.0 { t.Error("underdamped should overshoot") }
  }

  func BenchmarkSpringManagerTick20(b *testing.B) {
      sm := NewSpringManager()
      for i := 0; i < 20; i++ { sm.Set(fmt.Sprintf("s%d", i), float64(i)*10) }
      b.ResetTimer()
      for i := 0; i < b.N; i++ { sm.Tick() }
  }

## E2E / VHS Verification
Video review critical: ntm-dashboard-navigate for focus transitions, progress overshoot.

## Logging
- Log spring creation: "spring: %s stiffness=%.1f damping=%.1f deadzone=%.1f"
- Debug log active count per tick: "springs: %d active"
- Log tick rate change: "tick: %dms (springs active=%v)"
- Log dead zone suppression: "spring %s: delta %.2f within dead zone %.2f, skipping"

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SpringManager created (NO mutex — single-threaded)
- [ ] Dead zone prevents micro-oscillation animation
- [ ] Focus transitions spring-animated
- [ ] Progress bars spring to new values
- [ ] Sparklines spring-animate data updates
- [ ] ReducedMotion instant
- [ ] Tick rate increases when springs active
- [ ] All 7 tests + benchmark pass
- [ ] Benchmark: < 500ns per Tick() for 20 springs
- [ ] Video shows smooth animations

## Files to Create
- internal/tui/components/spring_manager.go
- internal/tui/components/spring_manager_test.go

## Files to Modify
- internal/tui/dashboard/update.go (tick management)
- internal/tui/dashboard/focus.go (focus spring)
- internal/tui/components/progress.go (value spring)
- internal/tui/components/sparkline.go (data spring)

 {"id":"br-3qs46","title":"Attention Feed Phase 1d3: Resolve bead_orphaned feasibility and honest unsupported-condition behavior","description":"# Why this bead exists
`bead_orphaned` is strategically useful but epistemically risky. ntm should not claim that work is orphaned unless it can defend that conclusion from real observable data. This bead exists to resolve that uncertainty deliberately instead of letting it hide inside a larger coordination bead.

# Scope
1. Determine whether `bead_orphaned` can be implemented honestly from ntm's real visibility into beads/bv/agent state.
2. If it can, define the exact observable criteria and signal payload.
3. If it cannot, define the exact unsupported / degraded behavior for commands, capabilities, and help output.
4. Ensure wait/digest/help/capabilities surfaces all tell the same truth about whether `bead_orphaned` is supported, partially supported, or unavailable.
5. Leave behind explicit rationale so future contributors know whether the limitation is conceptual, architectural, or just unimplemented.

# Design considerations
- This is the easiest coordination signal to fake badly.
- An honest `not supported` is better than a seductive but unreliable heuristic.
- The result of this bead should shape command behavior and documentation, not remain tribal knowledge.

# Likely touch points
- `internal/bv/`
- any beads integration surfaces in ntm

 {"id":"br-5u235","title":"Overlay-Feed T1: Add Attention Items panel to dashboard overlay","description":"# Add Attention Items Panel to Dashboard Overlay

## Background

The dashboard overlay (popup mode, F12) currently shows the same panels as the normal dashboard: pane list, agent detail, beads pipeline, alerts, activity/locks. None of these panels are aware of the Attention Feed event system.

The Attention Feed (being built under br-byln7) introduces a normalized event envelope with actionability levels: action_required, interesting, background, suppressed. The dashboard overlay should surface the action_required and interesting items in a dedicated panel so the human sees the same signals an operator agent would see.

## What To Build

### 1. New PanelAttention type
- Add PanelAttention to the panel enum in dashboard.go (alongside PanelPaneList, PanelDetail, etc.)
- Add it to the panel cycling order (Tab key navigation)
- Position it in the layout between alerts and activity panels
- The panel should have a header: "Attention" with an icon
- Register it in the layout tier system (layout.TierForWidth): visible at TierWide and TierSplit, hidden at TierNarrow and TierCompact to avoid breaking small terminals

### 2. Panel rendering (renderAttentionPanel method)
- Show the top N (default 10) attention items, each with:
  - Severity icon: red circle for action_required, yellow triangle for interesting
  - One-line summary (from the event envelope summary field)
  - Age in relative format (e.g., "2m ago", "just now") using the existing formatRelativeTime helper
  - Source pane identifier (e.g., "pane 3 (codex)")
- Items sorted by: actionability desc (action_required first), then recency desc
- Scrollable if more items than fit (use the existing cursor/viewport patterns)
- Each item must track which pane generated it (pane index) for zoom-to-source

### 3. Zoom-to-source action (Enter/z on attention item)
- When the attention panel is focused and the user presses Enter or z on a selected item, zoom to the source pane that generated the attention event
- This reuses the exact same zoom mechanism as PanelPaneList: call tmux.ZoomPane(session, paneIndex), optionally set postQuitAction
- In popup mode: zoom + close popup (same as pane list zoom behavior)
- If the source pane no longer exists (agent was respawned), show a brief health message: "Source pane no longer available"
- This makes the attention panel actionable, not just informational

### 4. Data source and refresh
- Add an attentionItems []AttentionItem field to the dashboard Model
- Add a fetchAttentionCmd() that reads from the Attention Feed journal service
- Refresh on the same interval as alerts (alertsRefreshInterval, typically 5s)
- The fetch should be a tea.Cmd that runs async (same pattern as fetchBeadsCmd)

### 5. Graceful degradation mechanism
- The fetchAttentionCmd should attempt to connect to the feed journal
- Detection: check if the journal singleton/service is initialized. The feed service will expose an IsReady() bool or similar method.
- If the feed is not ready: set a feedAvailable bool field to false on the Model
- When feedAvailable=false: render the panel as "Attention Feed not active" in dimmed text (theme.Current().Overlay color)
- When feedAvailable=true but items are empty: render "All clear" in green (same pattern as alerts panel empty state)
- Never crash, never panic, never show a Go error message to the user

### 6. Panel visibility
- The attention panel should be visible in BOTH popup mode and normal dashboard mode
- In popup mode, it is more prominent (first in the right panel group)
- In normal mode, it appears after alerts (same position, just less prominent)
- At TierNarrow and TierCompact, the panel is HIDDEN to preserve space for core panels (pane list, detail). The badge in the stats bar (T2) still shows attention count even when the panel is hidden, so the user knows to widen the terminal or check robot mode.

## Unit Test Requirements

- Test renderAttentionPanel with 0 items -> "All clear" message
- Test renderAttentionPanel with mixed actionability items -> correct sort order
- Test renderAttentionPanel with feed unavailable -> "not active" message
- Test panel appears in the cycling order when Tab is pressed (at wide tiers)
- Test panel is SKIPPED in cycling order at narrow tiers
- Test AttentionItem sorting: action_required before interesting, then by time desc
- Test age formatting uses formatRelativeTime correctly
- Test zoom-to-source: selecting an item with a valid pane index triggers ZoomPane
- Test zoom-to-source: selecting an item with an invalid pane index shows health message

## Technical Notes

- The AttentionItem struct should include: summary, actionability, timestamp, source_pane (int), source_agent_type, event_cursor (int64)
- The panel should NOT hold a reference to the journal — it should receive data via tea.Cmd responses (same as all other panels)
- The refresh command can be gated: only fetch if feed is available (avoid unnecessary work)
- The zoom-to-source follows the exact same code path as the existing zoom handler in dashKeys.Zoom, just reading the pane index from the attention item instead of from m.panes[m.cursor]

## Dependencies

- Depends on: Attention Feed Phase 1a (br-xmmaj) — normalized event envelope must exist
- Depends on: Attention Feed Phase 1c (br-04qv0) — derived attention signals must exist for meaningful data

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] PanelAttention exists and renders in the dashboard layout
- [ ] Panel shows attention items from the feed when available
- [ ] Panel gracefully degrades when feed is not available (no crash, clear message)
- [ ] Panel supports scrolling when items exceed available height
- [ ] Panel is in the Tab cycling order at wide tiers, hidden at narrow tiers
- [ ] Enter/z on an attention item zooms to the source pane
- [ ] Zoom-to-source handles missing panes gracefully
- [ ] Refresh follows async tea.Cmd pattern
- [ ] Unit tests for rendering in all three states (items, empty, unavailable)
- [ ] Unit tests for zoom-to-source (valid and invalid pane)
- [ ] Unit tests for tier visibility
- [ ] go build ./cmd/ntm succeeds
- [ ] go vet passes","notes":"Implemented attention feed startup cadence seeding and changed the dashboard fetch path to sort full actionable feed results before trimming to the top 10. Added regression coverage for startup cadence, top-10 ordering, shared attention-feed test isolation, and sidebar-space expectations after the attention panel is present.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:04:15.980397874Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T07:52:23.326802692Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T07:52:23.325509723Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["attention-feed","dashboard","overlay","phase-1","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-5u235","depends_on_id":"br-04qv0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:10:27.248703502Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-5u235","depends_on_id":"br-x66gc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:10:36.103968607Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-5u235","depends_on_id":"br-xmmaj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:10:26.184980976Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

## Background
NTM has interactive prompts using raw fmt.Scanf or custom Bubble Tea (init wizard, spawn
config, kill confirmation). huh provides styled forms with validation, custom themes, a11y.

## Implementation Plan

### 0. Add dependency
  go get github.com/charmbracelet/huh@latest

### 1. Catppuccin Huh theme adapter: internal/tui/theme/huh.go
  func CatppuccinHuhTheme() *huh.Theme {
      base := huh.ThemeBase()
      t := Current()
      base.Focused.Title = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(t.Text).Bold(true)
      base.Focused.Description = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(t.Subtext)
      base.Focused.SelectedOption = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(t.Primary)
      base.Focused.UnselectedOption = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(t.Overlay)
      // ... map all relevant styles
      return base
  }

### 2. Kill Confirmation (cli/kill.go)
  var confirm bool
  err := huh.NewConfirm().
      Title("Kill session '" + session + "'?").
      Description(fmt.Sprintf("This will terminate %d agents.", count)).
      Affirmative("Yes, kill all").
      Negative("Cancel").
      Value(&confirm).
      WithTheme(theme.CatppuccinHuhTheme()).
      Run()

### 3. Init Wizard (cli/init_wizard.go)
  Form with groups: project name, template selection, agent counts, confirm.

### 4. Theme Selection (config command)
  Select[string] with Mocha/Macchiato/Latte options.

### 5. Spawn Interactive (when no --cc/--cod/--gmi flags)
  Form with agent count inputs.

## Unit Tests (REQUIRED)

### Test file: internal/tui/theme/huh_test.go
  func TestCatppuccinHuhTheme(t *testing.T) {
      theme := CatppuccinHuhTheme()
      if theme == nil { t.Fatal("theme should not be nil") }
      // Verify focused title style has non-zero foreground
      if theme.Focused.Title.GetForeground() == lipgloss.NoColor{} {
          t.Error("focused title should have foreground color")
      }
  }

  func TestCatppuccinHuhThemeAllVariants(t *testing.T) {
      for _, name := range []string{"mocha", "macchiato", "latte"} {
          SetTheme(name)
          theme := CatppuccinHuhTheme()
          if theme == nil { t.Errorf("theme nil for %s", name) }
      }
  }

### Test file: internal/cli/kill_test.go (add/expand)
  func TestKillConfirmationText(t *testing.T) {
      // Verify the confirmation message includes session name and agent count
      msg := formatKillConfirmation("myproject", 5)
      if !strings.Contains(msg, "myproject") { t.Error("missing session name") }
      if !strings.Contains(msg, "5") { t.Error("missing agent count") }
  }

### Huh forms require interactive terminal — integration test pattern:
  func TestKillConfirmationHuhForm(t *testing.T) {
      if os.Getenv("NTM_TEST_INTERACTIVE") == "" {
          t.Skip("interactive test requires NTM_TEST_INTERACTIVE=1")
      }
      // Actually run the form...
  }

## E2E / VHS Verification
Create testdata/vhs/huh-kill-confirm.tape:
  Output testdata/vhs/output/huh-kill-confirm.gif
  Require ntm
  Type "ntm kill test_session"  Enter
  Sleep 2s
  Type "n"  # Select "Cancel"
  Sleep 1s
  Enter
Run manually to verify Catppuccin styling renders correctly.

## Logging
- Log form display: "huh: showing %s form (theme=%s)"
- Log form result: "huh: %s form result=%v"
- Log when form cancelled: "huh: %s form cancelled by user"

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] huh dependency added to go.mod
- [ ] CatppuccinHuhTheme() produces valid theme for all 3 variants
- [ ] Kill confirmation uses huh.Confirm with destructive styling
- [ ] Init wizard uses huh.Form with grouped fields
- [ ] All new unit tests pass
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] VHS shows styled forms with Catppuccin colors
- [ ] -f flag still bypasses confirmation (no form shown)

## Files to Modify

- internal/cli/init_wizard.go (huh form)
- internal/cli/spawn.go (interactive mode)

## Files to Create
- internal/tui/theme/huh.go (Catppuccin adapter)
- internal/tui/theme/huh_test.go (tests)

 {"id":"br-6tzh9","title":"Attention Feed Phase 3b: Ship --robot-digest as the default what-changed surface","description":"# Why this bead exists
`--robot-digest` should become the default answer to "what changed since I last looked?" for operator agents that do not need the full raw feed every time. This bead turns the digest engine into a polished robot-mode command.

# Scope
1. Add a canonical `--robot-digest` command with cursor-based replay semantics.
2. Return `cursor_start`, `cursor_end`, `summary`, digest buckets, and suppression counts.
3. Support the same filtering philosophy as the raw feed while keeping the surface simpler than full event inspection.
4. Return explicit resync guidance when the requested cursor is too old.
5. Ensure the command points naturally to `--robot-events` or more targeted robot surfaces for follow-up when needed.
6. Keep response shape stable enough that operator agents can rely on it as the default steady-state loop surface.

# Detailed verification expectations
- Golden tests for normal digest output and expired-cursor behavior.
- Tests that digest filters line up with the underlying engine.
- Tests proving the command can surface both raw-state churn suppression and high-value signals.
- Transcript-style scenario coverage for: snapshot -> digest -> attention -> digest follow-up.
- Detailed logs in tests that print summary text, bucket counts, suppression counts, and cursor handoff when expectations fail.

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `--robot-digest` exists and is useful as the default delta surface.
- [ ] The response shape makes cursor chaining and follow-up inspection obvious.
- [ ] Coordination-aware and core attention signals both appear correctly once available.
- [ ] The command reduces the need for repeated full snapshots in normal operator loops.
- [ ] Digest transcript scenarios are covered with detailed logs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:20.926802931Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T08:41:00.847819208Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T08:41:00.847252827Z","close_reason":"DigestOptions, DigestResponse, PrintDigest implemented in events.go with tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["attention-feed","cli","digest","phase-3","robot"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-6tzh9","depends_on_id":"br-3qs46","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:01:40.989461011Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-6tzh9","depends_on_id":"br-815ez","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:24.498489127Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-6tzh9","depends_on_id":"br-89wvt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:01:42.881169266Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-6tzh9","depends_on_id":"br-fsoxa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:24.805353019Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-6tzh9","depends_on_id":"br-kpvhy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:25.130344370Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-6tzh9","depends_on_id":"br-vdfjr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:01:40.607667325Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-7746h","title":"Attention Feed Phase 4b0: Build shared Go scenario harness, tmux fixtures, and artifact plumbing for transcript and e2e verification","description":"# Why this bead exists
Both the core attention-feed initiative and the overlay-feed integration branch now require Go-based end-to-end/transcript verification with rich artifacts. If each branch invents its own tmux fixture lifecycle, artifact pathing, timeout policy, and logging format, the result will be duplicated, inconsistent, and much harder to debug. This bead creates one shared Go verification harness so the later transcript/e2e beads can focus on scenarios instead of re-building plumbing.

# Scope
1. Build reusable Go helpers for tmux-backed scenario setup/teardown, including deterministic session naming, cleanup on failure, and explicit timeout handling.
2. Define one shared artifact strategy for transcript/e2e runs: directory layout, naming, timestamp format, retention/debug mode, and where stdout/stderr, cursor traces, rendered summaries, and transport captures should be written.
3. Provide reusable helpers for invoking ntm robot/CLI/network surfaces, capturing structured output, and annotating artifact timelines with scenario steps.
4. Provide reusable assertion/logging helpers for cursor handoff, wake reasons, degraded markers, focus targets, and other operator-loop invariants that multiple e2e beads will need.
5. Keep the harness implemented in Go and suitable for use from `e2e/` and integration-style tests; do not rely on shell-script runners as the canonical infrastructure.
6. Make the harness neutral enough that both the core attention-feed transcript bead and the overlay-feed transcript bead can reuse it without forking conventions.

# Design considerations
- The harness should reduce duplication, not become a mini framework that obscures what each scenario is doing.
- Cleanup must be robust enough that failed tmux-based tests do not leave session clutter behind.
- Artifact layout should optimize post-failure diagnosis first, not just “green test” terseness.
- Shared helpers must preserve enough raw detail that later failures can still be understood without stepping through the harness internals.

# Detailed verification expectations
- Unit tests for artifact path generation, timestamp formatting, retention/debug behavior, and cleanup helpers.
- Tests for helper behavior when tmux setup fails, command execution fails, or cleanup itself encounters missing resources.
- Example harness-driven smoke scenarios that prove artifacts are emitted in the documented structure.
- Detailed `t.Logf` output in harness tests showing artifact root, session IDs, cleanup decisions, and retained artifact locations when assertions fail.

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] A reusable Go scenario/artifact harness exists for transcript/e2e work.
- [ ] Both the core attention-feed and overlay-feed e2e/transcript beads can depend on it instead of inventing separate plumbing.
- [ ] Artifact layout, retention/debug mode, and cleanup behavior are explicit and tested.
- [ ] The harness improves diagnosability without forcing shell-script orchestration into the repo.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-21T01:23:46.362083521Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T02:43:08.191993090Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T02:43:08.191497012Z","close_reason":"Implemented shared Go scenario harness with: ArtifactManager for standardized artifact layout, ScenarioHarness for tmux-backed e2e, RobotResult with JSON helpers, CursorTraceEntry for cursor tracking, assertion helpers. All unit tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["attention-feed","e2e","harness","overlay","tests","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-7746h","depends_on_id":"br-aa0nj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:25:31.177950401Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"br-7dnb7","title":"Phase 3 VERIFY: Run Full TUI Inspector Suite — Structural Refactor Gate","description":"# Phase 3 Verification Gate

## Purpose
After all Phase 3 tasks complete, verify no regressions.

## Mandatory Steps

### 1. Build verification
  go build ./cmd/ntm 2>&1 | tee /tmp/gate3_build.log
  go vet ./internal/tui/... 2>&1 | tee /tmp/gate3_vet.log

### 2. Unit test regression check
  go test -v -count=1 -short ./internal/tui/... 2>&1 | tee /tmp/gate3_tests.log
  # Count passing tests
  PASS_COUNT=$(grep -c '^--- PASS' /tmp/gate3_tests.log)
  BASELINE_COUNT=$(grep -c '^--- PASS' /tmp/tui_test_baseline.log)
  echo "Tests: phase3=$PASS_COUNT baseline=$BASELINE_COUNT"
  # PASS_COUNT must be >= BASELINE_COUNT (may be higher due to new tests)
  if [ "$PASS_COUNT" -lt "$BASELINE_COUNT" ]; then
      echo "ERROR: Test count regression! $PASS_COUNT < $BASELINE_COUNT"
      exit 1
  fi

### 3. VHS capture suite
  bash /dp/tui_inspector/scripts/run_ntm_tui_suite.sh \
    --profiles ntm-dashboard-empty,ntm-dashboard-help,ntm-dashboard-navigate,ntm-dashboard-wide,ntm-palette \
    --suite-name "phase3_post_upgrade"

### 4. Snapshot comparison
  For each profile, compare phase3 vs previous phase snapshots.
  Log any visual differences.

### 5. Log summary

 {"id":"br-7h8it","title":"Attention Feed Phase 5: Validate the end-to-end operator loop and enforce rollout guardrails","description":"# Why this bead exists
The final question is not merely "did the code compile?" but "did ntm become a more agent-intuitive nervous system without drifting into a fake coordinator, and do the human-facing summary surfaces agree with that reality?" This bead is the capstone validation and rollout gate for the attention-feed initiative.

# Scope
1. Validate the implemented operator loop end to end:
   - cold start with snapshot
   - wait or attention against a known cursor
   - wake on meaningful change
   - inspect raw events only when needed
   - resync cleanly when replay is no longer possible
2. Confirm CLI, HTTP, SSE, and WebSocket parity where promised.
3. Confirm the discoverability surfaces (`--robot-help`, `--robot-capabilities`) accurately reflect reality.
4. Confirm dashboard and terse output reflect the same frontier as the operator-facing feed.
5. Confirm low-noise operator mode and high-fidelity debug mode both behave as intended.
6. Confirm the feature did not smuggle in a planner, second task graph, or hidden coordinator state.
7. Confirm both the deterministic unit/integration suite and the transcript-style e2e suite actually make failures diagnosable.
8. Record rollout guardrails in the implementation/docs/help output so future contributors understand the intended boundary of the feature.
9. Treat overlay-specific follow-on integration as separate work unless those beads are explicitly pulled into scope later; this gate is for the core attention-feed initiative.

# Validation checklist
- [ ] `go build ./cmd/ntm`
- [ ] `go test -short ./...`
- [ ] `go test -v ./e2e/...`
- [ ] relevant targeted package tests pass
- [ ] transcript-style e2e coverage for the new operator loop passes
- [ ] cursor expiry / resync behavior is clear and non-misleading
- [ ] operator loop feels simpler than repeated snapshot polling
- [ ] no schema drift between the promised contract and actual outputs
- [ ] no accidental architectural overreach into planning/coordinating logic
- [ ] help/capabilities/dashboard/terse output agree with the implemented command set
- [ ] operator/debug profile behavior is coherent and documented
- [ ] unit/integration logs and e2e artifacts are detailed enough to debug failures after the fact

# Artifact expectations
Closing this bead should leave behind a concise verification record identifying what commands/tests were run and where the most useful transcript or log artifacts can be found.","notes":"Validation complete: build passes, go test -short passes (verified assign, swarm, tui, webhook packages), e2e tests pass including all operator loop scenarios (snapshot bootstrap, wake on attention, raw event replay, profile comparison, error recovery, dashboard output).  {"id":"br-7kigk","title":"Attention Feed Phase 3c: Evolve --robot-wait into a condition-driven attention gate","description":"# Why this bead exists
`internal/robot/wait.go` already offers useful waiting behavior, but it is currently narrower and more polling-oriented than what an operator LLM needs. This bead evolves wait into a condition-driven attention gate that can sleep efficiently until specific kinds of change occur.

# Mandatory condition set
Existing conditions to preserve:
- `idle`
- `complete`
- `generating`
- `healthy`

New attention-oriented conditions to add:
- `attention`
- `action_required`
- `mail_pending`
- `mail_ack_required`
- `stalled`
- `rate_limited`
- `context_hot`
- `reservation_conflict`
- `file_conflict`
- `session_changed`
- `pane_changed`
- `bead_orphaned` (only if implemented honestly)

# Scope
1. Add named conditions and keep them enumerable/documented.
2. Add `--since-cursor` support so waiters can bind themselves to a known observation frontier.
3. Preserve sensible timeout, transition, and error semantics.
4. Return a machine-usable wake payload that includes the matched condition, relevant cursor boundary/hand-off information, and enough detail for a caller to move into digest/events follow-up without guessing.
5. Ensure wait decisions consume the same normalized feed/signal pipeline rather than duplicating state logic.
6. Explicitly reject a general expression language unless a clear need emerges later.
7. Make honest degradation explicit for conditions that are partially observable or intentionally unsupported in v1.

# Guardrails
- Keep the interface teachable to an LLM.
- Avoid condition semantics that depend on undocumented background polling behavior.
- If a condition cannot be implemented honestly, narrow it rather than shipping a misleading version.

# Detailed verification expectations
- Synthetic feed tests for each new condition.
- Timeout tests and transition-boundary tests.
- Tests for wake payload shape, matched-condition reporting, and cursor handoff semantics.
- Tests for honest degradation when a condition is unavailable or only partially observable.
- Scenario transcripts for wait wakeups and timeouts using snapshot-provided cursors.
- Detailed logs in tests for matched condition, timeout path, observed cursor range, returned wake payload, and ignored competing signals.

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Existing wait functionality is preserved.
- [ ] The new condition set is available, documented, and consistent with the feed taxonomy.
- [ ] `--since-cursor` works as a real boundary, not a cosmetic flag.
- [ ] Wait responses expose machine-usable wake payloads and cursor handoff semantics.
- [ ] Timeouts and error payloads remain machine-usable.
- [ ] Condition behavior is covered by transcript-style scenarios and detailed logs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:21.152265573Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T08:55:00.051078465Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T08:55:00.050088261Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["attention-feed","phase-3","robot","wait"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-7kigk","depends_on_id":"br-04qv0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:25.486200475Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-7kigk","depends_on_id":"br-3qs46","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:01:41.750052444Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-7kigk","depends_on_id":"br-89wvt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:01:43.271960296Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-7kigk","depends_on_id":"br-fsoxa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:25.820885564Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-7kigk","depends_on_id":"br-vdfjr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:01:41.383112878Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-7v4ns","title":"Attention Feed Phase 1b: Map existing bus/tracker/session signals into one normalized feed service","description":"# Why this bead exists
A journal alone is not useful if each producer speaks a different dialect. ntm already emits data from several places: event bus notifications, tracker state changes, session/pane lifecycle transitions, alerts, activity monitors, and other robot helpers. This bead makes those sources legible to a robot client through one internal mapping/query layer.

# Scope
1. Map existing tracker change types and bus events into the normalized envelope.
2. Decide which fields should be filled for each source: category, type, summary, details, source, and candidate `next_actions`.
3. Route session and pane lifecycle events through the same path used for replay and live subscription.
4. Create one shared query/subscription service so `--robot-events`, `--robot-digest`, wait conditions, and server streaming all consume the same underlying feed.
5. Keep raw output out of the high-level envelope unless it is genuinely required; the feed should tell an operator what changed, not dump pane text continuously.
6. Document unmapped or intentionally suppressed source events in code/tests so future contributors understand what was omitted and why.

# Design considerations
- Summary strings should be compact but concrete, because they are what an LLM will read most often.
- `details` should hold structured fields that later commands can use mechanically.
- `next_actions` must never point at imaginary commands; they should reference real robot-mode follow-ups such as `--robot-tail`, `--robot-context`, `--robot-status`, or the new feed surfaces.
- Avoid duplicating mapping logic in both CLI and server code; parity depends on one source of truth.

# Detailed verification expectations
- Mapping tests for tracker changes, bus events, session creation/removal, pane creation/removal, alerts, and activity changes.
- Tests that validate `next_actions` only reference real supported surfaces.
- Fixture coverage table that lists each input event family and the expected normalized output shape.
- Debug logging in tests for mapping misses or intentionally dropped events.

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Existing ntm signals are representable through one normalized mapping path.
- [ ] Replay and live subscription go through the same mapping logic.
- [ ] Event summaries/details are specific enough to guide follow-up inspection without requiring raw logs.
- [ ] The implementation does not fork into separate CLI-only and server-only schemas.
- [ ] Unmapped or intentionally omitted signals are explicit rather than accidental.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:19.637988898Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T03:11:04.602754573Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T03:11:04.602174267Z","close_reason":"Added shared AttentionFeed publish helpers for tracker/logged/bus sources, live bus subscription, and mapping/service tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["attention-feed","events","mapping","phase-1","robot"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-7v4ns","depends_on_id":"br-xmmaj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T00:49:22.564595243Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
